content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 21, 2003

Jim Dickens, Mayor Pro Tem
City of Arroyo Grande
Post Office Box 550
Arroyo Grande, CA 93421

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-252

Dear Mr. Dickens:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a)(copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act as mandated by section 83111.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 – 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 03-252
Page No. 2

Should you have any questions, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [illegible]
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
JS:jg
